name: Pasta with Sauted Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 3 cups prepared raw vegetables (such as onion, pepper, squash, mushrooms, eggplant, spinach, or chard)
1 large tomato, peeled and seeded, then chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1 gallon of water to a boil in a large pot.
2. Add dried pasta and cook until al dente (just soft enough that it still needs to be chewed).
3. Drain the pasta in a colander.
4. For fresh pasta, turn the heat down to low, cover the pot, and keep the water warm until the vegetables are almost done before cooking the pasta.
5. In a large skillet, add oil and garlic. Turn the heat to medium and cook the garlic just until it begins to color (do not let it burn or become bitter).
6. Add the vegetables in layers of how long they will take to cook, starting with those that need the longest time. Stir constantly with a spatula so they do not overcook.
7. For fresh pasta, reboil the water at this point and cook the pasta for its final minutes while cooking the vegetables.
8. Add the herbs to the vegetables and mix well to distribute them.
9. When the vegetables are almost cooked (still slightly crunchy), add canned tomato and crushed pepper flakes. Turn the heat down to low and simmer for 3-4 minutes to blend the flavors.
10. Taste and add kosher salt if needed.
